title: FinalizerOut
description:
Schema for the JSON object returned by `prompts/observed_answer_fallback_prompt.md` (and other finalize-tier classifiers that share the same envelope). Mirrors `ObservedAnswerFallbackOut` in `crates/clawd/src/agent_engine/observed_output.rs` and is enforced by the `finalizer_out_schema_drift` unit test in the same file.

§D2 (schema-driven prompt I/O 第三份样板, after intent_normalizer + plan_result): the finalizer always returns a flat object with `answer` text plus 5 boolean / numeric quality flags that the caller cross-checks against `semantic_judge` only on low confidence (< 0.55). All flags must be present (`#[serde(default)]` covers missing keys, but the schema documents the canonical shape).

answer.description: Final user-facing text. Empty string is allowed only when `qualified=false` and `needs_clarify=true`.

qualified.description: True iff observed outputs are sufficient for a direct final answer (caller's `qualified_completion` disposition).

needs_clarify.description: True iff observed outputs truly cannot answer the request and a clarification turn is required.

is_meta_instruction.description: True iff the answer is still a meta/placeholder ('I will check/read/inspect first') instead of a real final answer. Caller drops the answer and falls back when this is true.

publishable.description: True iff `answer` is directly suitable for user delivery as the final answer (semantically OK to publish).

confidence.description: Self-rated confidence in [0,1]. Below `OBSERVED_SELF_CLASSIFY_CONF_THRESHOLD` (= 0.55) the caller calls `semantic_judge::is_meta_respond_instruction` to double-check.

reason.description: Short, concrete justification. Currently consumed only by audit log (renamed to `_reason` on the Rust side because the runtime body does not branch on it).
